REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND EXPENSES FILED BY RESPONDENT THE ROULAC GROUP
POS OF PETITIONER'S SUR-REPLY IN OPPOSITION TO AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND EXPENSES FILED BY PETITIONER GWIRE, WILLIAM

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT THE ROULAC GROUP'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND EXPENSES. RULING: COUNSEL APPEARED TO SEEK CLARIFICATION OF THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH WAS AS FOLLOWS: "DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO FILE A TIMELY COST MEMORANDUM, THAT IS WITHIN 15 DAYS AFTER MAILING OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT. CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1700 (A)(1). IN ADDITION, THE ATTORNEY'S FEES PROVISION IS IN AN AGREEMENT TO INDEMNIFY AND THERE HAS BEEN NO DETERMINATION THAT PLAINTIFF HAS AN OBLIGATION TO INDEMNIFY DEFENDANT." ARGUED AND THE COURT CLARIFIED THAT THE APPLICABLE FEE PROVISION IS NOT AN INDEMNIFICATION PROVISION THAT IS APPLICABLE, IT IS THE PROVISION IN PARAGRAPH 4. IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION THAT THE SUM OF $5,775.00 IS RECOVERABLE. THAT THE OTHER AMOUNTS SOUGHT, ASSUMING THAT THERE WAS A TIMELY REQUEST, DO NOT CONSTITUTE ATTORNEYS FEES THAT WOULD BE RECOVERABLE UNDER THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. PETITIONER TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Oct-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT THE ROULAC GROUP'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND EXPENSES.
APPEARANCES: Tia Pollastrini, Esq. of Gwire Law Offices 296-8880 appearing specially for William Gwire, Esq., petitioner & opposing party; Robin Gonzalez, Esq. 794-1856 appearing specially for J. Philip Martin, Esq. (794-1856) for respondent & moving party.
RULING: Counsel appeared to seek clarification of the Court's tentative ruling, which was as follows: "Denied. Defendant failed to file a timely cost memorandum, that is within 15 days after mailing of notice of entry of judgment. California Rule of Court 3.1700 (a)(1). In addition, the attorney's fees provision is in an agreement to indemnify and there has been no determination that plaintiff has an obligation to indemnify defendant."
Argued and the Court clarified that the applicable fee provision is not an indemnification provision that is applicable, it is the provision in paragraph 4. It is the Court's conclusion that the sum of $5,775.00 is recoverable. That the other amounts sought, assuming that there was a timely request, do not constitute attorneys fees that would be recoverable under the agreement of the parties. Petitioner to prepare proposed order. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
